content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 curiosity-driven active learning agents can leverage a dynamic, query-based compression gate that only compresses and summarizes long-term memory when the agent’s intrinsic curiosity reward for unexplored experience exceeds a learned threshold. This creates a 'curiosity-aligned memory archive' that retains high-uncertainty, learning-relevant details in full while compressing predictable, low-value experiences into concise summaries.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
